title: Isla and the Case of the Missing Carrots
synopsis: When carrots start disappearing from the community garden, young detective Isla teams up with her animal friends to solve the mystery. Through careful observation and teamwork, they discover the surprising truth behind the missing vegetables.

pages.text: Isla loved the community garden. Every morning, she walked there with her mom. The garden had big red tomatoes, green beans, and orange carrots. Today something was wrong. "Mom, look!" said Isla. "The carrots are gone!"

illustration.description: A young girl with curly brown hair and dark skin points at empty holes in garden soil where carrots should be. Her mother, wearing a sun hat, looks surprised. The garden is lush and green with various vegetables growing in neat rows under bright morning sunlight.
illustration.alt: Isla pointing at empty carrot holes in the garden

pages.text: Mrs. Chen was watering her beans. "Oh no!" she said. "My carrots are missing too!" Mr. Ahmed looked at his empty carrot patch. "This is very strange," he said. "Who could take all our carrots?"

illustration.description: Three adults of different ethnicities stand around empty garden plots looking confused and concerned. Mrs. Chen holds a watering can, Mr. Ahmed wears gardening gloves, and Isla's mom has her hands on her hips. The empty soil shows small holes where carrots were pulled out.
illustration.alt: The gardeners discovering their missing carrots

pages.text: Isla put on her thinking cap. Well, not a real cap. She just thought really hard. "I will solve this mystery!" she said. "I will find our carrots!" The grown-ups smiled. "Good luck, Detective Isla," said Mrs. Chen.

illustration.description: Isla strikes a confident detective pose with her hands on her hips and a determined expression. Her big brown eyes sparkle with excitement. The adults smile warmly at her in the background, and the morning sun creates a cheerful, encouraging atmosphere.

pages.text: Isla got down on her hands and knees. She looked at the dirt very carefully. "Aha!" she said. "Tiny footprints!" The footprints were very small. Much smaller than people feet. They looked like little paws.

illustration.description: Isla crouches close to the ground examining tiny paw prints in the dark soil. Her face shows intense concentration and excitement as she discovers the first clue. Magnifying glass effect shows the small animal tracks clearly in the dirt.
illustration.alt: Isla examining small paw prints in the soil

pages.text: "These look like rabbit prints," said Isla. She followed the tiny tracks. They went around the tomatoes. They went past the beans. They went all the way to the old oak tree. "The mystery rabbit went this way!" Isla said.

illustration.description: Isla follows a trail of small paw prints that wind through the garden like a dotted line. She walks carefully between tall tomato plants and bean poles, following the tracks toward a large oak tree in the distance. Her expression shows focused determination.
illustration.alt: Isla following the paw print trail through the garden

pages.text: At the oak tree, Isla met Squeak the mouse. "Hi Squeak!" said Isla. "Did you see who took our carrots?" Squeak twitched his whiskers. "I saw something orange this morning," he said. "But I was too sleepy to look closely."

illustration.description: A tiny gray mouse with big expressive eyes sits on a tree root talking to Isla. Squeak looks sleepy with droopy eyelids, while Isla leans in to listen carefully. The oak tree's massive trunk creates a cozy backdrop with dappled sunlight filtering through leaves.
illustration.alt: Isla talking to Squeak the mouse by the oak tree

pages.text: Chirp the robin flew down from her nest. "I saw something too!" she said. "Early this morning, I saw a brown blur near the carrots. It was moving very fast!" Isla wrote this down in her notebook. Well, she drew a picture of a brown blur.

illustration.description: A cheerful red-breasted robin with bright orange chest feathers perches on Isla's outstretched finger. Isla holds a small notebook and pencil, drawing squiggly lines to represent the 'brown blur.' The robin looks animated and helpful, with wings slightly spread.
illustration.alt: Chirp the robin telling Isla about the brown blur

pages.text: Buzz the bee buzzed over to help. "Bzzzz! I know about that brown blur!" she said. "It had long ears and a fluffy tail. It was hopping very fast!" Now Isla was getting somewhere. The mystery animal had long ears, a fluffy tail, and could hop!

illustration.description: A plump yellow and black striped bee hovers near Isla's ear, animated and excited to share information. Isla's face lights up with understanding as she connects the clues. Her notebook now shows simple drawings of long ears and a fluffy tail.
illustration.alt: Buzz the bee giving Isla more clues

pages.text: "It sounds like a rabbit!" said Isla. "But why would a rabbit take ALL the carrots? That's a lot of carrots for one little rabbit." Squeak, Chirp, and Buzz looked at each other. "Maybe the rabbit was very hungry?" suggested Chirp.

illustration.description: Isla stands thinking with one finger on her chin, surrounded by her three animal friends. Squeak sits on her shoulder, Chirp perches on a garden stake, and Buzz hovers nearby. All four characters have puzzled but determined expressions as they work together.
illustration.alt: Isla and her animal friends pondering the mystery

pages.text: The friends followed the paw prints deeper into the woods behind the garden. "Look!" said Isla. "Orange pieces!" There were small bits of carrot on the ground. The trail was getting stronger. "We're getting close!" said Buzz excitedly.

illustration.description: Isla and her animal friends venture into a sun-dappled forest with tall green trees. Orange carrot pieces are scattered on the forest floor like breadcrumbs leading deeper into the woods. The lighting is magical with golden sunbeams filtering through the canopy.
illustration.alt: Following carrot pieces into the forest

pages.text: They came to a small clearing. In the middle was a big burrow under some roots. "Hello?" called Isla softly. "Is anyone home?" A small brown nose peeked out. Then two long ears. Then a whole rabbit appeared! But this rabbit looked different.

illustration.description: A cozy forest clearing surrounds a rabbit burrow nestled between large tree roots. A brown rabbit with gentle eyes cautiously emerges from the burrow entrance. Isla kneels nearby with a kind, non-threatening posture, while her animal friends watch curiously from behind her.
illustration.alt: Discovering the rabbit at her burrow

pages.text: The rabbit was bigger than Isla expected. And she had a very round belly. "Please don't be mad," said the rabbit. "My name is Rosie. I took the carrots, but I had a good reason!" Isla sat down to listen. She wasn't mad. She was curious!

illustration.description: Rosie the rabbit sits with a noticeably round belly, looking apologetic but hopeful. Her ears droop slightly with worry. Isla sits cross-legged on the forest floor with an understanding and gentle expression, ready to listen. The scene feels warm and non-judgmental.
illustration.alt: Rosie the rabbit explaining herself to Isla

pages.text: "I'm going to have babies soon," said Rosie. "I needed extra food to be strong and healthy. I was so hungry, and the carrots smelled so good!" Isla's heart felt warm. "Oh, Rosie! You're going to be a mama!"

illustration.description: Rosie looks relieved and happy as she explains, with one paw gently resting on her round belly. Isla's expression shows complete understanding and joy. Soft, warm lighting creates a tender moment between them, with the forest providing a peaceful backdrop.
illustration.alt: Rosie explaining she's going to have babies

pages.text: "But I felt bad about taking them," Rosie continued. "I was going to tell everyone, but I was too scared. I thought people would be angry with me." "We're not angry," said Isla. "We just want to help! Let's go talk to the gardeners together."

illustration.description: Isla extends her hand toward Rosie in a gesture of friendship and support. Rosie looks hopeful but still a bit nervous. Squeak, Chirp, and Buzz nod encouragingly in the background. The scene conveys acceptance, understanding, and the beginning of a solution.
illustration.alt: Isla offering to help Rosie talk to the gardeners

pages.text: Back at the garden, everyone listened to Rosie's story. "Of course we'll help!" said Mrs. Chen. "We always share with our forest friends." Mr. Ahmed smiled. "We'll plant extra carrots just for you and your babies, Rosie!" Mystery solved, and everyone was happy!

illustration.description: The entire group stands together in the sunny garden - Isla, her mom, Mrs. Chen, Mr. Ahmed, and Rosie the rabbit, with the small animal friends nearby. Everyone has warm, welcoming smiles. The scene radiates community spirit and kindness, with the lush garden as a perfect backdrop.
illustration.alt: Everyone welcoming Rosie and offering to help

metadata.illustrationStyle: Playful storybook illustration style. Rich saturated colors. Expressive characters with big eyes. Whimsical, like Pixar concept art.

questions.question: What was missing from the garden?
questions.explanation: The carrots were missing from everyone's garden plots.

questions.question: What clues did Isla find in the dirt?
questions.explanation: Isla found tiny paw prints that looked like they came from small animal paws.

questions.question: Which animal friend saw a brown blur?
questions.explanation: Chirp the robin saw a brown blur moving fast near the carrots early in the morning.

questions.question: Why did Rosie take the carrots?
choices: She was going to have babies
questions.explanation: Rosie was going to have babies and needed extra food to stay strong and healthy.

questions.question: How did the gardeners help Rosie?
choices: They offered to plant extra carrots for her
questions.explanation: The gardeners were kind and understanding, offering to plant extra carrots just for Rosie and her babies.

pages.instructions: Help complete the story by choosing the right words!
pages.text: Isla loved the community _____. Every morning, she walked there with her mom. The garden had big red tomatoes, green _____, and orange carrots. Today something was wrong. The _____ were gone!

pages.instructions: Choose the best words to finish what Rosie said!
pages.text: I'm going to have _____ soon, said Rosie. I needed extra food to be strong and _____. I was so hungry, and the carrots smelled so _____!
